article_title: Aalo Atomics Completes First Nuclear Reactor Built on DOE Land in 50 Years, Targeting Criticality Within Weeks

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: Aalo Atomics Completes First Nuclear Reactor Built on DOE Land in 50 Years, Targeting Criticality Within Weeks
content_preview.summary: Austin-based Aalo Atomics unveiled its completed Critical Test Reactor at Idaho National Laboratory on March 20, built in just 36 days, as the startup races to achieve criticality ahead of the DOE's July 4 deadline.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Aalo Atomics, an Austin, Texas-based nuclear startup founded in 2023, unveiled its completed Critical Test Reactor at Idaho National Laboratory on March 20, 2026 — the first new nuclear reactor constructed on Department of Energy land in half a century. The facility was built in 36 days and the reactor assembled in 40 days, a construction pace that contrasts sharply with the decade-long timelines typical of conventional nuclear projects.

The company is now awaiting final DOE approv...

editor_notes.content_quality: Detailed nuclear energy article covering Aalo Atomics' rapid reactor construction timeline and commercial ambitions. Good technical depth on XMR design and sodium cooling. Well-contextualized within DOE Reactor Pilot Program.
editor_notes.source_verification: Interesting Engineering (fetched): Confirms 5 reactor modules, Austin-to-INL shipment Dec 2025, Loszak CEO '1000x scale' quote, sodium 100x heat removal, XMR category, Aalo Pod 50MW, atmospheric pressure, air-cooled condensers. DOE (fetched): Confirms INL Materials and Fuels Complex site, MARVEL design inspiration, Arafat CTO 'proving ground' quote, 10MW sodium-cooled. BusinessWire #1 (timeout): Could not fetch but URL structure consistent with press release format. BusinessWire #2 (not fetched): Baker Hughes turbine details consistent with company's disclosed timeline.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Key claims verified: INL site, MARVEL inspiration, sodium cooling, XMR concept, Loszak and Arafat quotes, factory-built modules. The 36-day build and 40-day assembly claims are company statements. Internal link to March 2 microreactor article confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Appropriately notes uncertainties about commercial timeline ($136M funding, GigaWatt Factory by 2028).
editor_notes.originality: No prior Aalo Atomics coverage. Complements March 2 microreactor race article.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong human-requested nuclear energy submission. Key sources verified, internal link confirmed. Ready for publication.
